title: pseudognaphalium schraderi
intro: Pseudognaphalium schraderi, also known as Schrader's buttonweed, is a flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, and its health benefits may include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, Pseudognaphalium schraderi is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
